payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: tools/bench/probe_hidden_buffer.py
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Probe hidden activations and emit MCP-style JSONL docs."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import hashlib
+import json
+import re
+import sys
+from pathlib import Path
+from typing import Any, Dict, Iterable, List, Sequence, Tuple
+
+from model_utils import HFController
+
+
+def _wire_probe_imports(repo_root: Path) -> None:
+    probe_root = repo_root / "trm_hidden_buffer_mcp_probe"
+    if not probe_root.exists():
+        raise RuntimeError(
+            "Missing trm_hidden_buffer_mcp_probe directory. "
+            "Expected at repo root."
+        )
+    if str(probe_root) not in sys.path:
+        sys.path.insert(0, str(probe_root))
+
+
+def iter_jsonl(path: Path) -> Iterable[Dict[str, Any]]:
+    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
+        for line in handle:
+            row = line.strip()
+            if not row:
+                continue
+            try:
+                yield json.loads(row)
+            except json.JSONDecodeError:
+                continue
+
+
+def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
+    parser.add_argument("--model-path", type=str, required=True, help="HF base model path.")
+    parser.add_argument("--adapter-path", type=str, default="", help="Optional PEFT adapter path.")
+    parser.add_argument("--device", type=str, default="cpu", help='Transformers device_map, e.g. "auto" or "cpu".')
+    parser.add_argument("--dtype", type=str, default="auto", choices=["auto", "float16", "bfloat16", "float32"])
+    parser.add_argument("--require-gpu", action="store_true", help="Fail if CUDA is unavailable or model is not on GPU.")
+    parser.add_argument("--allow-large-model", action="store_true", help="Allow models above the size cap.")
+    parser.add_argument("--max-model-params-millions", type=float, default=200.0, help="Reject larger models unless --allow-large-model.")
+    parser.add_argument("--dataset", type=str, default="", help="Optional JSONL dataset (uses 'input' field as prompt).")
+    parser.add_argument("--prompt", type=str, default="", help="Optional single prompt.")
+    parser.add_argument("--max-rows", type=int, default=32, help="Cap prompts from dataset (0 = all).")
+    parser.add_argument("--max-frames", type=int, default=4096, help="Ring buffer frame capacity.")
+    parser.add_argument(
+        "--hook-regex",
+        type=str,
+        default=r"self_attn\.o_proj$,mlp\.down_proj$,post_attention_layernorm$",
+        help="Comma-separated regex patterns matched against module names.",
+    )
+    parser.add_argument("--max-hooks", type=int, default=24, help="Max modules to hook.")
+    parser.add_argument("--out", type=str, default="tools/bench/out/hidden_buffer_docs.jsonl")
+    parser.add_argument("--run-id", type=str, default="")
+    return parser.parse_args()
+
+
+def _collect_prompts(args: argparse.Namespace) -> List[Tuple[int, str]]:
+    prompts: List[Tuple[int, str]] = []
+    if args.prompt:
+        prompts.append((0, args.prompt))
+
+    if args.dataset:
+        dataset = Path(args.dataset)
+        count = 0
+        for idx, row in enumerate(iter_jsonl(dataset)):
+            prompt = row.get("input")
+            if not isinstance(prompt, str) or not prompt.strip():
+                continue
+            prompts.append((idx, prompt))
+            count += 1
+            if args.max_rows and count >= args.max_rows:
+                break
+    return prompts
+
+
+def _compile_patterns(pattern_csv: str) -> Sequence[re.Pattern[str]]:
+    patterns = []
+    for part in pattern_csv.split(","):
+        s = part.strip()
+        if s:
+            patterns.append(re.compile(s))
+    return patterns
+
+
+def _pick_hook_specs(model: Any, max_hooks: int, hook_regex: str):
+    from trm_hidden_buffer_probe.hooks import HookSpec
+
+    patterns = _compile_patterns(hook_regex)
+    candidates: List[Tuple[str, Any]] = []
+    for name, module in model.named_modules():
+        if not name:
+            continue
+        if any(p.search(name) for p in patterns):
+            candidates.append((name, module))
+    if not candidates:
+        raise RuntimeError(
+            f"No modules matched hook patterns: {hook_regex}. "
+            "Inspect model.named_modules() and adjust --hook-regex."
+        )
+
+    if len(candidates) > max_hooks:
+        step = max(1, len(candidates) // max_hooks)
+        sampled = candidates[::step][:max_hooks]
+    else:
+        sampled = candidates
+
+    return [HookSpec(name=name, module=module, extractor=lambda _m, _i, o: o) for name, module in sampled]
+
+
+def _prompt_hash(prompt: str) -> str:
+    return hashlib.sha256(prompt.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:16]
+
+
+def main() -> int:
+    args = parse_args()
+    repo_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
+    _wire_probe_imports(repo_root)
+
+    from trm_hidden_buffer_probe.buffer import RingBuffer
+    from trm_hidden_buffer_probe.hooks import ActivationTap
+    from trm_hidden_buffer_probe.mcp.index import MCPIndexStore
+    from trm_hidden_buffer_probe.summaries import summarize_frames
+    import torch
+
+    prompts = _collect_prompts(args)
+    if not prompts:
+        raise RuntimeError("No prompts provided. Use --prompt or --dataset.")
+
+    controller = HFController(
+        model_path=args.model_path,
+        adapter_path=(args.adapter_path or None),
+        device=args.device,
+        dtype=args.dtype,
+        require_gpu=args.require_gpu,
+        allow_large_model=args.allow_large_model,
+        max_model_params_millions=args.max_model_params_millions,
+    )
+    model = controller.model
+    tokenizer = controller.tokenizer
+    model.eval()
+
+    run_id = args.run_id or f"probe-{Path(args.model_path).name}"
+    ring = RingBuffer(max_frames=args.max_frames)
+    tap = ActivationTap(ring)
+    specs = _pick_hook_specs(model, args.max_hooks, args.hook_regex)
+    tap.install(specs)
+
+    store = MCPIndexStore()
+    for i, (src_idx, prompt) in enumerate(prompts):
+        tap.bump_step(i)
+        inputs = tokenizer(prompt, return_tensors="pt")
+        model_device = getattr(model, "device", None)
+        if model_device is not None and str(model_device) != "meta":
+            inputs = {k: v.to(model_device) for k, v in inputs.items()}
+
+        with torch.inference_mode():
+            _ = model(**inputs, use_cache=False)
+
+        # Each hook emits one frame per forward pass for the current step.
+        new_frames = [f for f in ring.latest(k=len(specs) * 2) if f.step == i]
+        summaries = summarize_frames(new_frames)
+        for summary in summaries:
+            store.add_summary(
+                run_id=run_id,
+                summary=summary,
+                extra_meta={
+                    "prompt_idx": int(src_idx),
+                    "prompt_hash": _prompt_hash(prompt),
+                    "prompt_chars": len(prompt),
+                },
+            )
+        if (i + 1) % 10 == 0:
+            print(f"[probe] prompts={i + 1} docs={len(store.docs)}")
+
+    out_path = Path(args.out)
+    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    store.dump_jsonl(str(out_path))
+    tap.remove()
+
+    print(f"Wrote {out_path} with {len(store.docs)} docs from {len(prompts)} prompts.")
+    return 0
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    raise SystemExit(main())
*** End Patch

usage: probe_hidden_buffer.py [-h] --model-path MODEL_PATH
                              [--adapter-path ADAPTER_PATH] [--device DEVICE]
                              [--dtype {auto,float16,bfloat16,float32}]
                              [--require-gpu] [--allow-large-model]
                              [--max-model-params-millions MAX_MODEL_PARAMS_MILLIONS]
                              [--dataset DATASET] [--prompt PROMPT]
                              [--max-rows MAX_ROWS] [--max-frames MAX_FRAMES]
                              [--hook-regex HOOK_REGEX]
                              [--max-hooks MAX_HOOKS] [--out OUT]
                              [--run-id RUN_ID]

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --model-path MODEL_PATH
                        HF base model path.
  --adapter-path ADAPTER_PATH
                        Optional PEFT adapter path.
  --device DEVICE       Transformers device_map, e.g. "auto" or "cpu".
  --dtype {auto,float16,bfloat16,float32}
  --require-gpu         Fail if CUDA is unavailable or model is not on GPU.
  --allow-large-model   Allow models above the size cap.
  --max-model-params-millions MAX_MODEL_PARAMS_MILLIONS
                        Reject larger models unless --allow-large-model.
  --dataset DATASET     Optional JSONL dataset (uses 'input' field as prompt).
  --prompt PROMPT       Optional single prompt.
  --max-rows MAX_ROWS   Cap prompts from dataset (0 = all).
  --max-frames MAX_FRAMES
                        Ring buffer frame capacity.
  --hook-regex HOOK_REGEX
                        Comma-separated regex patterns matched against module
                        names.
  --max-hooks MAX_HOOKS
                        Max modules to hook.
  --out OUT
  --run-id RUN_ID

payload.message:
Completed in `C:\projects\StoryworldTRM\TRMStoryworld`.

Changes made:
- Added `tools/bench/probe_hidden_buffer.py`
- Updated `tools/bench/README.md` with probe usage

What this gives you:
- Loads your HF/PEFT controller model via existing harness logic.
- Installs forward hooks from your scaffold (`trm_hidden_buffer_mcp_probe`).
- Captures activations into ring buffer, summarizes them, and writes MCP-style JSONL docs.
- Works from dataset prompts (`input` field) or a single `--prompt`.

Sanity checks run:
- Scaffold demo ran successfully:
  - `python -m trm_hidden_buffer_probe.examples.minimal_torch_hook`
  - wrote `trm_hidden_buffer_mcp_probe/demo_mcp_docs.jsonl`
- New script CLI check passed:
  - `python tools/bench/probe_hidden_buffer.py --help`

Recommended hook sites for your current harness:
- Your model artifacts indicate Llama-style block names (`...layers.N...`), so hook:
1. `self_attn.o_proj`
2. `mlp.down_proj`
3. `post_attention_layernorm`

That is already the default in `tools/bench/probe_hidden_buffer.py` via:
- `--hook-regex "self_attn\.o_proj$,mlp\.down_proj$,post_attention_layernorm$"`

Run it:
```powershell
python tools/bench/probe_hidden_buffer.py `
  --model-path <base_model> `
  --adapter-path <adapter_optional> `
  --dataset <controller_sft.jsonl> `
  --max-rows 64 `
  --device cpu `
  --out tools/bench/out/hidden_buffer_docs.jsonl
```

If you want, next I can add a companion script that correlates these activation docs with eval outcomes from `tools/bench/out/eval_summary.json` to identify which hook sites best predict failures.

+        rows_out_path = Path(args.rows_out)
+        rows_out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+        with rows_out_path.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
+            for row in per_row:
+                handle.write(json.dumps(row, ensure_ascii=True) + "\n")
     print(json.dumps(summary, indent=2))
     return 0
*** End Patch

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: C:/projects/StoryworldTRM/TRMStoryworld/tools/bench/correlate_hidden_buffer_with_eval.py
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Correlate hidden-buffer docs with eval outcomes and rank informative modules."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import json
+from pathlib import Path
+from typing import Any, Dict, Iterable, List, Optional, Sequence, Tuple
+
+import numpy as np
+
+
+def iter_jsonl(path: Path) -> Iterable[Dict[str, Any]]:
+    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
+        for line in handle:
+            row = line.strip()
+            if not row:
+                continue
+            try:
+                yield json.loads(row)
+            except json.JSONDecodeError:
+                continue
+
+
+def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
+    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
+    p.add_argument("--hidden-docs", required=True, help="JSONL from probe_hidden_buffer.py")
+    p.add_argument("--eval-rows-jsonl", default="", help="Preferred per-row outcomes JSONL from eval_trm_controller.py --rows-out")
+    p.add_argument("--eval-summary-json", default="", help="Fallback summary JSON with samples[row,target,pred_pick]")
+    p.add_argument(
+        "--failure-mode",
+        choices=["invalid_or_incorrect", "incorrect_only", "invalid_only"],
+        default="invalid_or_incorrect",
+    )
+    p.add_argument("--run-id", default="", help="Optional run_id filter for hidden docs")
+    p.add_argument("--min-samples", type=int, default=8, help="Minimum rows per module to score")
+    p.add_argument("--top-k", type=int, default=20)
+    p.add_argument("--out-json", default="tools/bench/out/hidden_buffer_correlation.json")
+    p.add_argument("--out-md", default="tools/bench/out/hidden_buffer_correlation.md")
+    return p.parse_args()
+
+
+def load_hidden_docs(path: Path, run_id: str) -> Dict[int, Dict[str, np.ndarray]]:
+    by_row: Dict[int, Dict[str, np.ndarray]] = {}
+    for doc in iter_jsonl(path):
+        meta = doc.get("meta", {})
+        if run_id and str(meta.get("run_id", "")) != run_id:
+            continue
+        row = meta.get("prompt_idx")
+        name = meta.get("name")
+        emb = doc.get("embedding")
+        if row is None or name is None or not isinstance(emb, list):
+            continue
+        try:
+            row_i = int(row)
+            vec = np.asarray(emb, dtype=np.float32)
+        except Exception:
+            continue
+        if vec.size == 0:
+            continue
+        by_row.setdefault(row_i, {})[str(name)] = vec
+    return by_row
+
+
+def load_outcomes_from_rows(path: Path, failure_mode: str) -> Dict[int, bool]:
+    out: Dict[int, bool] = {}
+    for row in iter_jsonl(path):
+        idx = row.get("row")
+        if idx is None:
+            continue
+        try:
+            idx_i = int(idx)
+        except Exception:
+            continue
+        valid = bool(row.get("valid_pick"))
+        correct = bool(row.get("correct"))
+        out[idx_i] = is_failure(valid=valid, correct=correct, mode=failure_mode)
+    return out
+
+
+def load_outcomes_from_summary(path: Path, failure_mode: str) -> Dict[int, bool]:
+    raw = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+    out: Dict[int, bool] = {}
+    for s in raw.get("samples", []):
+        idx = s.get("row")
+        if idx is None:
+            continue
+        try:
+            idx_i = int(idx)
+        except Exception:
+            continue
+        pred_pick = s.get("pred_pick")
+        target = s.get("target")
+        valid = pred_pick is not None
+        correct = valid and (target is not None) and (int(pred_pick) == int(target))
+        out[idx_i] = is_failure(valid=valid, correct=correct, mode=failure_mode)
+    return out
+
+
+def is_failure(valid: bool, correct: bool, mode: str) -> bool:
+    if mode == "invalid_only":
+        return not valid
+    if mode == "incorrect_only":
+        return valid and (not correct)
+    return (not valid) or (not correct)
+
+
+def cosine_distance(a: np.ndarray, b: np.ndarray, eps: float = 1e-8) -> float:
+    denom = (np.linalg.norm(a) * np.linalg.norm(b)) + eps
+    return float(1.0 - (float(np.dot(a, b)) / denom))
+
+
+def auc_from_scores(pos: Sequence[float], neg: Sequence[float]) -> float:
+    if not pos or not neg:
+        return 0.5
+    better = 0.0
+    total = 0
+    for p in pos:
+        for n in neg:
+            total += 1
+            if p > n:
+                better += 1.0
+            elif p == n:
+                better += 0.5
+    return float(better / max(total, 1))
+
+
+def cohen_d(a: np.ndarray, b: np.ndarray, eps: float = 1e-8) -> float:
+    if a.size < 2 or b.size < 2:
+        return 0.0
+    a_var = float(np.var(a, ddof=1))
+    b_var = float(np.var(b, ddof=1))
+    pooled = ((a.size - 1) * a_var + (b.size - 1) * b_var) / max(a.size + b.size - 2, 1)
+    return float((np.mean(a) - np.mean(b)) / (np.sqrt(pooled) + eps))
+
+
+def score_modules(by_row: Dict[int, Dict[str, np.ndarray]], outcomes: Dict[int, bool], min_samples: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
+    modules = sorted({m for row_map in by_row.values() for m in row_map.keys()})
+    results: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
+    for module in modules:
+        fail_vecs: List[np.ndarray] = []
+        succ_vecs: List[np.ndarray] = []
+        for row, row_map in by_row.items():
+            if row not in outcomes:
+                continue
+            vec = row_map.get(module)
+            if vec is None:
+                continue
+            (fail_vecs if outcomes[row] else succ_vecs).append(vec)
+        n_fail = len(fail_vecs)
+        n_succ = len(succ_vecs)
+        n = n_fail + n_succ
+        if n < min_samples or n_fail == 0 or n_succ == 0:
+            continue
+        fail_mat = np.stack(fail_vecs, axis=0)
+        succ_mat = np.stack(succ_vecs, axis=0)
+        mu_fail = fail_mat.mean(axis=0)
+        mu_succ = succ_mat.mean(axis=0)
+
+        direction = mu_fail - mu_succ
+        denom = float(np.linalg.norm(direction)) + 1e-8
+        direction = direction / denom
+        fail_scores = (fail_mat @ direction).astype(np.float32)
+        succ_scores = (succ_mat @ direction).astype(np.float32)
+
+        auc = auc_from_scores(fail_scores.tolist(), succ_scores.tolist())
+        d = cohen_d(fail_scores, succ_scores)
+        cdist = cosine_distance(mu_fail, mu_succ)
+        signal = max(abs(auc - 0.5) * 2.0, abs(d) / 3.0, cdist)
+
+        results.append(
+            {
+                "module": module,
+                "n": n,
+                "n_fail": n_fail,
+                "n_success": n_succ,
+                "auc_fail_vs_success": round(float(auc), 6),
+                "effect_size_d": round(float(d), 6),
+                "centroid_cosine_distance": round(float(cdist), 6),
+                "signal_score": round(float(signal), 6),
+                "fail_mean_l2": round(float(np.linalg.norm(mu_fail)), 6),
+                "success_mean_l2": round(float(np.linalg.norm(mu_succ)), 6),
+            }
+        )
+    results.sort(key=lambda r: r["signal_score"], reverse=True)
+    return results
+
+
+def write_markdown(path: Path, args: argparse.Namespace, rows_overlap: int, module_scores: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
+    lines = [
+        "# Hidden Buffer Correlation Report",
+        "",
+        f"- hidden_docs: `{args.hidden_docs}`",
+        f"- eval_rows_jsonl: `{args.eval_rows_jsonl or '(none)'}`",
+        f"- eval_summary_json: `{args.eval_summary_json or '(none)'}`",
+        f"- failure_mode: `{args.failure_mode}`",
+        f"- joined_rows: `{rows_overlap}`",
+        "",
+        "## Top modules",
+        "",
+        "| rank | module | signal | auc | d | centroid_cos_dist | n | n_fail | n_success |",
+        "|---:|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|",
+    ]
+    for i, row in enumerate(module_scores[: args.top_k], start=1):
+        lines.append(
+            "| {rank} | `{module}` | {signal:.4f} | {auc:.4f} | {d:.4f} | {cd:.4f} | {n} | {nf} | {ns} |".format(
+                rank=i,
+                module=row["module"],
+                signal=row["signal_score"],
+                auc=row["auc_fail_vs_success"],
+                d=row["effect_size_d"],
+                cd=row["centroid_cosine_distance"],
+                n=row["n"],
+                nf=row["n_fail"],
+                ns=row["n_success"],
+            )
+        )
+    path.write_text("\n".join(lines) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
+
+
+def main() -> int:
+    args = parse_args()
+    hidden_path = Path(args.hidden_docs)
+    if not hidden_path.exists():
+        raise FileNotFoundError(f"hidden docs not found: {hidden_path}")
+
+    by_row = load_hidden_docs(hidden_path, args.run_id)
+    if not by_row:
+        raise RuntimeError("No hidden docs loaded. Check --hidden-docs and --run-id.")
+
+    outcomes: Dict[int, bool] = {}
+    if args.eval_rows_jsonl:
+        rows_path = Path(args.eval_rows_jsonl)
+        if not rows_path.exists():
+            raise FileNotFoundError(f"eval rows jsonl not found: {rows_path}")
+        outcomes = load_outcomes_from_rows(rows_path, args.failure_mode)
+    elif args.eval_summary_json:
+        summary_path = Path(args.eval_summary_json)
+        if not summary_path.exists():
+            raise FileNotFoundError(f"eval summary json not found: {summary_path}")
+        outcomes = load_outcomes_from_summary(summary_path, args.failure_mode)
+    else:
+        raise RuntimeError("Provide --eval-rows-jsonl (preferred) or --eval-summary-json.")
+
+    overlap_rows = sorted(set(by_row.keys()).intersection(outcomes.keys()))
+    if not overlap_rows:
+        raise RuntimeError("No row overlap between hidden docs and eval outcomes.")
+
+    by_row_overlap = {k: by_row[k] for k in overlap_rows}
+    module_scores = score_modules(by_row_overlap, outcomes, args.min_samples)
+    if not module_scores:
+        raise RuntimeError(
+            "No modules met scoring criteria. Reduce --min-samples or collect more probe/eval rows."
+        )
+
+    out_json = Path(args.out_json)
+    out_md = Path(args.out_md)
+    out_json.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    out_md.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+    payload = {
+        "config": {
+            "hidden_docs": args.hidden_docs,
+            "eval_rows_jsonl": args.eval_rows_jsonl,
+            "eval_summary_json": args.eval_summary_json,
+            "failure_mode": args.failure_mode,
+            "run_id_filter": args.run_id,
+            "min_samples": args.min_samples,
+            "top_k": args.top_k,
+        },
+        "joined_rows": len(overlap_rows),
+        "ranked_modules": module_scores,
+    }
+    out_json.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
+    write_markdown(out_md, args, len(overlap_rows), module_scores)
+
+    print(f"Wrote {out_json} and {out_md}. Top module: {module_scores[0]['module']}")
+    return 0
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    raise SystemExit(main())
*** End Patch

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: C:/projects/StoryworldTRM/TRMStoryworld/tools/bench/README.md
@@
 ## Eval controller
 ```powershell
 python tools/bench/eval_trm_controller.py --dataset <controller_sft.jsonl> --model-path <base_model> --adapter-path <adapter> --device cpu
 ```
 
+With per-row outcome export (recommended for hidden-buffer correlation):
+```powershell
+python tools/bench/eval_trm_controller.py `
+  --dataset <controller_sft.jsonl> `
+  --model-path <base_model> `
+  --adapter-path <adapter_optional> `
+  --device cpu `
+  --out tools/bench/out/eval_summary.json `
+  --rows-out tools/bench/out/eval_rows.jsonl
+```
+
 ## Rollout eval
 ```powershell
 python tools/bench/eval_storyworld_rollout.py --swmd <story.swmd.min.md> --episodes 16 --max-steps 12 --base-model <base_model> --adapter-path <adapter> --device cpu
 ```
@@
 Notes:
 - Hooks are selected by module name regex; default targets are Llama-style:
   - `self_attn.o_proj`
   - `mlp.down_proj`
   - `post_attention_layernorm`
 - Override with `--hook-regex`.
 - Output is MCP-style JSONL docs for retrieval/indexing workflows.
+
+## Correlate hidden buffer with eval failures
+```powershell
+python tools/bench/correlate_hidden_buffer_with_eval.py `
+  --hidden-docs tools/bench/out/hidden_buffer_docs.jsonl `
+  --eval-rows-jsonl tools/bench/out/eval_rows.jsonl `
+  --failure-mode invalid_or_incorrect `
+  --out-json tools/bench/out/hidden_buffer_correlation.json `
+  --out-md tools/bench/out/hidden_buffer_correlation.md
+```
+
+Notes:
+- This ranks modules by how strongly their activation summaries separate failure vs success rows.
+- If you only have summary data, use `--eval-summary-json`; row-level JSONL is preferred.
*** End Patch

payload.message:
Implemented.

### Added
- `tools/bench/correlate_hidden_buffer_with_eval.py`

### Updated
- `tools/bench/eval_trm_controller.py`
  - new `--rows-out` to export per-row outcomes JSONL for correlation
- `tools/bench/README.md`
  - added commands for `--rows-out`
  - added correlation workflow section

### What the correlator does
- Joins hidden-buffer docs (`prompt_idx` + module embeddings) with eval outcomes (`row`).
- Labels failures using configurable mode:
  - `invalid_or_incorrect` (default)
  - `incorrect_only`
  - `invalid_only`
- Ranks modules by failure-separation signal:
  - `auc_fail_vs_success`
  - `effect_size_d`
  - `centroid_cosine_distance`
  - combined `signal_score`
- Writes:
  - JSON: `tools/bench/out/hidden_buffer_correlation.json`
  - Markdown report: `tools/bench/out/hidden_buffer_correlation.md`

### Run sequence
1. Generate per-row eval outcomes:
```powershell
python tools/bench/eval_trm_controller.py `
  --dataset <controller_sft.jsonl> `
  --model-path <base_model> `
  --adapter-path <adapter_optional> `
  --device cpu `
  --out tools/bench/out/eval_summary.json `
  --rows-out tools/bench/out/eval_rows.jsonl
```

2. Generate hidden-buffer docs:
```powershell
python tools/bench/probe_hidden_buffer.py `
  --model-path <base_model> `
  --adapter-path <adapter_optional> `
  --dataset <controller_sft.jsonl> `
  --max-rows 64 `
  --device cpu `
  --out tools/bench/out/hidden_buffer_docs.jsonl
```

3. Correlate:
```powershell
python tools/bench/correlate_hidden_buffer_with_eval.py `
  --hidden-docs tools/bench/out/hidden_buffer_docs.jsonl `
  --eval-rows-jsonl tools/bench/out/eval_rows.jsonl `
  --failure-mode invalid_or_incorrect `
  --out-json tools/bench/out/hidden_buffer_correlation.json `
  --out-md tools/bench/out/hidden_buffer_correlation.md
```

If you want next, I can add a small plotter (`module signal bar chart + auc/effect scatter`) that reads `hidden_buffer_correlation.json`.

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: C:/projects/StoryworldTRM/TRMStoryworld/tools/bench/plot_hidden_buffer_correlation.py
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Plot hidden-buffer correlation outputs."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import json
+from pathlib import Path
+from typing import Any, Dict, List
+
+
+def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
+    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
+    p.add_argument(
+        "--in-json",
+        type=str,
+        default="tools/bench/out/hidden_buffer_correlation.json",
+        help="Input JSON from correlate_hidden_buffer_with_eval.py",
+    )
+    p.add_argument(
+        "--out-dir",
+        type=str,
+        default="tools/bench/out",
+        help="Directory for generated plots.",
+    )
+    p.add_argument("--top-k", type=int, default=20, help="Top modules to show in bar plot.")
+    return p.parse_args()
+
+
+def _require_matplotlib():
+    try:
+        import matplotlib.pyplot as plt  # type: ignore
+    except Exception as exc:
+        raise RuntimeError("matplotlib is required. Install with: pip install matplotlib") from exc
+    return plt
+
+
+def _load_payload(path: Path) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+    if not path.exists():
+        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Input JSON not found: {path}")
+    return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+
+
+def _bar_signal(plt, rows: List[Dict[str, Any]], out_path: Path, top_k: int) -> None:
+    top = rows[: max(top_k, 1)]
+    labels = [r["module"] for r in top][::-1]
+    vals = [float(r["signal_score"]) for r in top][::-1]
+
+    height = max(4, min(0.35 * len(labels) + 1.5, 18))
+    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, height))
+    ax.barh(labels, vals, color="#2a9d8f")
+    ax.set_title("Hidden Buffer Module Signal Ranking")
+    ax.set_xlabel("signal_score")
+    ax.grid(axis="x", linestyle="--", alpha=0.35)
+    fig.tight_layout()
+    fig.savefig(out_path, dpi=150)
+    plt.close(fig)
+
+
+def _scatter_auc_effect(plt, rows: List[Dict[str, Any]], out_path: Path) -> None:
+    auc = [float(r["auc_fail_vs_success"]) for r in rows]
+    eff = [float(r["effect_size_d"]) for r in rows]
+    sig = [float(r["signal_score"]) for r in rows]
+
+    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 7))
+    sc = ax.scatter(auc, eff, c=sig, cmap="viridis", alpha=0.8, edgecolors="none")
+    ax.axvline(0.5, color="gray", linestyle="--", linewidth=1)
+    ax.axhline(0.0, color="gray", linestyle="--", linewidth=1)
+    ax.set_title("Module Separation: AUC vs Effect Size")
+    ax.set_xlabel("AUC (failure > success projection)")
+    ax.set_ylabel("Cohen's d")
+    cbar = fig.colorbar(sc, ax=ax)
+    cbar.set_label("signal_score")
+    fig.tight_layout()
+    fig.savefig(out_path, dpi=150)
+    plt.close(fig)
+
+
+def main() -> int:
+    args = parse_args()
+    in_path = Path(args.in_json)
+    out_dir = Path(args.out_dir)
+    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    payload = _load_payload(in_path)
+    rows = payload.get("ranked_modules", [])
+    if not isinstance(rows, list) or not rows:
+        raise RuntimeError("No ranked_modules found in input JSON.")
+
+    plt = _require_matplotlib()
+
+    bar_path = out_dir / "hidden_buffer_signal_topk.png"
+    scatter_path = out_dir / "hidden_buffer_auc_vs_effect.png"
+    _bar_signal(plt, rows, bar_path, args.top_k)
+    _scatter_auc_effect(plt, rows, scatter_path)
+    print(f"Wrote {bar_path} and {scatter_path}")
+    return 0
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    raise SystemExit(main())
*** End Patch
